# `packages.wheels.dev` Static Site Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Ship `packages.wheels.dev` as a fifth Astro static site alongside `wheels.dev` / `blog.wheels.dev` / `guides.wheels.dev` / `api.wheels.dev`, fed by the `wheels-dev/wheels-packages` registry, rebuilt automatically on registry merges via `repository_dispatch`.

**Architecture:** Build-time data fetching from the GitHub-hosted registry (no runtime). Astro site at `web/sites/packages/` deploys to Cloudflare Pages project `wheels-packages` via the existing matrix in `.github/workflows/web-deploy.yml`. READMEs render through `unified` + `remark` + `rehype-sanitize` + `rehype-shiki`. A new `notify-site.yml` workflow in the registry repo fires `repository_dispatch` at the wheels repo on manifest merges; the deploy workflow gates registry-triggered runs to the `packages` matrix slot only.

**Tech Stack:** Astro 5, `@wheels-dev/ui` (workspace shared UI), TypeScript, unified/remark/rehype, `@shikijs/rehype`, `@astrojs/sitemap`, vitest (new — first unit-test runner in `web/`), Cloudflare Pages, GitHub Actions.

**Spec:** [`docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-23-wheels-packages-phase4-site-design.md`](../specs/2026-04-23-wheels-packages-phase4-site-design.md).

**Issue:** [#2271](https://github.com/wheels-dev/wheels/issues/2271). This plan closes the wheels.dev half (the in-app half shipped in #2277).

---

## File structure

**Files created (all under `web/`):**
- `web/sites/packages/package.json`
- `web/sites/packages/astro.config.mjs`
- `web/sites/packages/tsconfig.json`
- `web/sites/packages/public/favicon.svg` (copy from landing)
- `web/sites/packages/public/robots.txt`
- `web/sites/packages/src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro`
- `web/sites/packages/src/lib/registry.ts`
- `web/sites/packages/src/lib/registry.test.ts`
- `web/sites/packages/src/lib/markdown.ts`
- `web/sites/packages/src/lib/markdown.test.ts`
- `web/sites/packages/src/components/PackageCard.astro`
- `web/sites/packages/src/pages/index.astro`
- `web/sites/packages/src/pages/[name].astro`
- `web/tests/visual-baselines/packages-index.png` (captured, not handwritten)
- `web/tests/visual-baselines/packages-detail.png` (captured, not handwritten)
- `web/vitest.config.ts` — workspace-root vitest config

**Files modified:**
- `web/package.json` — add `vitest` devDep, add `test` script
- `web/pnpm-lock.yaml` — regenerated by pnpm install
- `.github/workflows/web-deploy.yml` — add `packages` to matrix, add `repository_dispatch` trigger, add matrix gate

**Cross-repo file created (separate PR, different repo):**
- `wheels-dev/wheels-packages/.github/workflows/notify-site.yml`
- `wheels-dev/wheels-packages/CONTRIBUTING.md` — append one-time setup note for `NOTIFY_WHEELS_TOKEN`

**Out of scope of this plan (manual one-time prep, noted in the PR description):**
- Create fine-grained PAT (`NOTIFY_WHEELS_TOKEN`) scoped to `wheels-dev/wheels` with `contents:write`; store in `wheels-dev/wheels-packages` as a repo secret.
- Create Cloudflare Pages project `wheels-packages`, attach `packages.wheels.dev` custom domain.

## Task 1: Scaffold packages site skeleton

**Files:**
- Create: `web/sites/packages/package.json`
- Create: `web/sites/packages/astro.config.mjs`
- Create: `web/sites/packages/tsconfig.json`

- Create: `web/sites/packages/src/pages/index.astro` (placeholder — real content in Task 5)

### Steps

- [ ] **Step 1.1: Create package.json**

```json
{
	"name": "@wheels-dev/site-packages",
	"version": "0.0.0",
	"private": true,
	"type": "module",
	"scripts": {
		"dev": "astro dev --port 4325",
		"build": "astro build",
		"preview": "astro preview",
		"check": "astro check",
		"test": "vitest run"
	},
	"dependencies": {
		"@astrojs/sitemap": "^3.7.2",
		"@shikijs/rehype": "^3.3.0",
		"@wheels-dev/ui": "workspace:*",
		"astro": "^5.1.0",
		"rehype-sanitize": "^6.0.0",
		"rehype-stringify": "^10.0.1",
		"remark-gfm": "^4.0.1",
		"remark-parse": "^11.0.0",
		"remark-rehype": "^11.1.2",
		"unified": "^11.0.5"
	},
	"devDependencies": {
		"@astrojs/check": "^0.9.4",
		"typescript": "^5.7.2",
		"vitest": "^2.1.9"
	}
}
```

- [ ] **Step 1.2: Create astro.config.mjs**

```js
import { defineConfig } from 'astro/config';
import sitemap from '@astrojs/sitemap';

export default defineConfig({
  site: 'https://packages.wheels.dev',
  integrations: [sitemap()],
});
```

- [ ] **Step 1.3: Create tsconfig.json**

Copy the existing file verbatim from the blog site:

```bash
cp web/sites/blog/tsconfig.json web/sites/packages/tsconfig.json
```

- [ ] **Step 1.4: Create public assets**

```bash
cp web/sites/blog/public/favicon.svg web/sites/packages/public/favicon.svg 2>/dev/null || \
  cp web/sites/landing/public/favicon.svg web/sites/packages/public/favicon.svg
```

If neither source exists, create a minimal 1-line placeholder SVG:

```bash
cat > web/sites/packages/public/favicon.svg <<'SVG'
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 32 32"><circle cx="16" cy="16" r="14" fill="#222"/></svg>
SVG
```

Create robots.txt:

```
User-agent: *
Allow: /

Sitemap: https://packages.wheels.dev/sitemap-index.xml
```

- [ ] **Step 1.5: Create BaseLayout.astro**

`web/sites/packages/src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro`:

```astro
---
import Header from '@wheels-dev/ui/components/Header.astro';
import Footer from '@wheels-dev/ui/components/Footer.astro';
import '@wheels-dev/ui/styles/tokens.css';
import '@wheels-dev/ui/styles/base.css';

interface Props {
  title: string;
  description?: string;
}

const { title, description = 'Wheels packages registry' } = Astro.props;
---
<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
  <head>
    <meta charset="utf-8" />
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />
    <title>{title}</title>
    <meta name="description" content={description} />
    <link rel="icon" type="image/svg+xml" href="/favicon.svg" />
  </head>
  <body>
    <Header />
    <main>
      <slot />
    </main>
    <Footer />
  </body>
</html>
```

**Note:** `@wheels-dev/ui`'s `Header`/`Footer` import paths are taken from `web/packages/ui/package.json` `exports`. If a build error reports a missing export, grep `web/packages/ui/package.json` and match the exact path from the `exports` map.

- [ ] **Step 1.6: Create placeholder index.astro**

```astro
---
import BaseLayout from '../layouts/BaseLayout.astro';
---
<BaseLayout title="Wheels packages">
  <section class="container">
    <h1>Wheels packages</h1>
    <p>Placeholder — real content in Task 5.</p>

</BaseLayout>
```

```bash
cd web
pnpm install
```

Expected: `pnpm-lock.yaml` updates, no errors about missing workspace packages. `@wheels-dev/ui` resolves via workspace protocol.

- [ ] **Step 1.8: Verify build succeeds**

```bash
pnpm --filter @wheels-dev/site-packages build
```

Expected: Astro builds `dist/`, no errors, no warnings about missing imports.

- [ ] **Step 1.9: Commit**

```bash
git add web/sites/packages web/pnpm-lock.yaml
git commit -m "feat(docs): scaffold packages.wheels.dev Astro site"
```

## Task 2: Set up vitest at workspace root

**Files:**
- Modify: `web/package.json`
- Create: `web/vitest.config.ts`

Vitest is the first unit-test runner in `web/`. Placing `vitest` at the workspace root (rather than per-site) means future sites can add tests without re-solving the setup problem. Each site inherits the runner via its own `"test": "vitest run"` script.

### Steps

- [ ] **Step 2.1: Add vitest to workspace root devDeps**

Edit `web/package.json` `devDependencies` (sorted alphabetically):

```json
	"devDependencies": {
		"pixelmatch": "^7.1.0",
		"playwright": "^1.59.1",
		"pngjs": "^7.0.0",
		"prettier": "^3.4.2",
		"prettier-plugin-astro": "^0.14.1",
		"typescript": "^5.7.2",
		"vitest": "^2.1.9"
	}
```

Add to `scripts` (sorted alphabetically, before `visual:*`):

```json
		"test": "pnpm -r --filter './sites/*' test"
```

So the full `scripts` block becomes:

```json
	"scripts": {
		"build": "pnpm -r --filter './sites/*' build",
		"dev:landing": "pnpm --filter @wheels-dev/site-landing dev",
		"dev:blog": "pnpm --filter @wheels-dev/site-blog dev",
		"dev:guides": "pnpm --filter @wheels-dev/site-guides dev",
		"dev:api": "pnpm --filter @wheels-dev/site-api dev",
		"format": "prettier --write \"**/*.{astro,ts,tsx,md,json,css}\"",
		"format:check": "prettier --check \"**/*.{astro,ts,tsx,md,json,css}\"",
		"test": "pnpm -r --filter './sites/*' test",
		"visual:test": "node scripts/visual-regression.mjs",
		"visual:baseline": "node scripts/visual-regression.mjs --update"
	},
```

(No need to add `"dev:packages"` now — can be added in a follow-up commit when the site has real content. Or add it alongside the other dev scripts in this step for consistency. Preference: add it, one less follow-up.)

Add `"dev:packages": "pnpm --filter @wheels-dev/site-packages dev"` after `dev:api`.

- [ ] **Step 2.2: Create vitest.config.ts at workspace root**

`web/vitest.config.ts`:

```ts
import { defineConfig } from 'vitest/config';

export default defineConfig({
  test: {
    environment: 'node',
    include: ['sites/*/src/**/*.test.ts'],
    exclude: ['**/node_modules/**', '**/dist/**'],
  },
});
```

(Per-site configs inherit this via the workspace. If a site needs a different environment — e.g. `jsdom` — it can add its own `vitest.config.ts` at site root overriding this.)

- [ ] **Step 2.3: Install the new devDep**

```bash
cd web
pnpm install
```

Expected: `vitest` and its transitive deps land in `node_modules`, no errors.

- [ ] **Step 2.4: Verify vitest runs (no tests yet → 0 passed, 0 failed)**

From `web/sites/packages/`:

```bash
cd web/sites/packages
pnpm test
```

Expected output contains: `No test files found`. Exit code: 0 (vitest `run` exits 0 when no test files match — confirm locally; if it exits 1, add `--passWithNoTests` flag to the package.json script).

If vitest exits 1 with "No test files found", change `package.json` script to:

```json
		"test": "vitest run --passWithNoTests"
```

- [ ] **Step 2.5: Commit**

```bash
git add web/package.json web/vitest.config.ts web/sites/packages/package.json web/pnpm-lock.yaml
git commit -m "feat(docs): add vitest to web workspace for unit tests"
```

(Use `docs` scope per the existing commit history — this change lives under `web/` which is docs-adjacent. If CI's commitlint rejects, reword to `chore(docs)` — but the existing `.github/workflows/web-deploy.yml` edits land as `feat(docs)` in history, so `feat(docs)` is fine.)

## Task 3: Implement `registry.ts` (TDD)

**Files:**
- Create: `web/sites/packages/src/lib/registry.ts`

### Steps

- [ ] **Step 3.1: Write failing test — listPackageNames happy path**

Create `web/sites/packages/src/lib/registry.test.ts`:

```ts
import { afterEach, beforeEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from 'vitest';

describe('registry', () => {
  const fetchSpy = vi.spyOn(globalThis, 'fetch');

  afterEach(() => {
    fetchSpy.mockReset();
  });

  describe('listPackageNames', () => {
    it('returns directory names from GH contents API, sorted and filtered', async () => {
      fetchSpy.mockResolvedValueOnce(
        new Response(
          JSON.stringify([
            { name: 'wheels-sentry', type: 'dir' },
            { name: 'wheels-hotwire', type: 'dir' },
            { name: 'README.md', type: 'file' },
          ]),
          { status: 200 },
        ),
      );

      const { listPackageNames } = await import('./registry');
      const names = await listPackageNames();

      expect(names).toEqual(['wheels-hotwire', 'wheels-sentry']);
      expect(fetchSpy).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
        'https://api.github.com/repos/wheels-dev/wheels-packages/contents/packages?ref=main',
        expect.anything(),
      );
    });
  });
});
```

- [ ] **Step 3.2: Run test, confirm it fails**

```bash
cd web/sites/packages
pnpm test
```

Expected: FAIL — `Cannot find module './registry'` or similar.

- [ ] **Step 3.3: Implement `listPackageNames`**

Create `web/sites/packages/src/lib/registry.ts`:

```ts
export interface ManifestVersion {
  version: string;
  publishedAt: string;
  wheelsVersion: string;
  sourceTag: string;
  tarball: string;
  sha256: string;
}

export interface Manifest {
  name: string;
  description: string;
  homepage?: string;
  tags?: string[];
  versions: ManifestVersion[];
}

export interface PackageSummary {
  name: string;
  description: string;
  homepage: string;
  tags: string[];
  latestVersion: string;
  publishedAt: string;
}

const REPO = process.env.WHEELS_PACKAGES_REGISTRY ?? 'wheels-dev/wheels-packages';
const BRANCH = 'main';

const UA = 'wheels-dev/packages-site (https://packages.wheels.dev)';

async function getJson(url: string): Promise<unknown> {
  const res = await fetch(url, { headers: { 'User-Agent': UA } });
  if (!res.ok) {
    throw new Error(`Registry fetch failed: ${res.status} ${res.statusText} — ${url}`);
  }
  return res.json();
}

async function getText(url: string): Promise<string> {
  const res = await fetch(url, { headers: { 'User-Agent': UA } });
  if (!res.ok) {
    throw new Error(`Registry fetch failed: ${res.status} ${res.statusText} — ${url}`);
  }
  return res.text();
}

export async function listPackageNames(): Promise<string[]> {
  const url = `https://api.github.com/repos/${REPO}/contents/packages?ref=${BRANCH}`;
  const entries = (await getJson(url)) as Array<{ name: string; type: string }>;
  return entries
    .filter((e) => e.type === 'dir')
    .map((e) => e.name)
    .sort();
}
```

- [ ] **Step 3.4: Run test, confirm it passes**

```bash
pnpm test
```

Expected: PASS (1 test).


```ts
        expect.anything(),
      );
    });

    it('throws on non-200', async () => {
      fetchSpy.mockResolvedValueOnce(new Response('not found', { status: 404 }));
```

- [ ] **Step 3.10: Run tests, confirm `fetchManifest` fails**

```bash
pnpm test
```

Expected: 2 new tests FAIL (`fetchManifest is not a function`).

- [ ] **Step 3.11: Implement `fetchManifest`**

Append to `registry.ts`:

```ts
export async function fetchManifest(name: string): Promise<Manifest> {
  const url = `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/${REPO}/${BRANCH}/packages/${name}/manifest.json`;
  const manifest = (await getJson(url)) as Manifest;
  if (!manifest || typeof manifest !== 'object' || !manifest.name || !Array.isArray(manifest.versions)) {
    throw new Error(`Malformed manifest for '${name}'`);
  }
  return manifest;
}
```

- [ ] **Step 3.12: Run tests**

```bash
pnpm test
```

Expected: 5 pass.

- [ ] **Step 3.15: Implement `fetchReadme`**

Append to `registry.ts`:

```ts
export async function fetchReadme(name: string): Promise<string> {
  const url = `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/${REPO}/${BRANCH}/packages/${name}/README.md`;
  return getText(url);
}
```

```ts
                { version: '1.1.0', publishedAt: '2026-04-01T00:00:00Z', wheelsVersion: '>=4.0', sourceTag: 'v1.1.0', tarball: 'x', sha256: 'y' },

              ],

            { status: 200 },

      const all = await listAll();

      expect(all[0].tags).toEqual([]);
    });
  });
```

- [ ] **Step 3.18: Run tests, confirm `listAll` fails**

```bash
pnpm test
```

Expected: 2 new tests FAIL.

- [ ] **Step 3.19: Implement `listAll`**

Append to `registry.ts`:

```ts
export async function listAll(): Promise<PackageSummary[]> {
  const names = await listPackageNames();
  const manifests = await Promise.all(names.map(fetchManifest));
  return manifests.map((m) => {
    const latest = m.versions[m.versions.length - 1];
    return {
      name: m.name,
      description: m.description ?? '',
      homepage: m.homepage ?? '',
      tags: m.tags ?? [],
      latestVersion: latest.version,
      publishedAt: latest.publishedAt,
    };
  });
}
```

- [ ] **Step 3.20: Run tests**

```bash
pnpm test
```

Expected: 9 pass.

```bash
git add web/sites/packages/src/lib/registry.ts web/sites/packages/src/lib/registry.test.ts
git commit -m "feat(docs): add registry.ts build-time fetcher for packages site"
```

## Task 4: Implement `markdown.ts` (TDD)

**Files:**
- Create: `web/sites/packages/src/lib/markdown.ts`
- Create: `web/sites/packages/src/lib/markdown.test.ts`

### Steps

- [ ] **Step 4.1: Write failing test — basic markdown renders**

Create `web/sites/packages/src/lib/markdown.test.ts`:

```ts
import { describe, expect, it } from 'vitest';

describe('renderMarkdown', () => {
  it('renders basic markdown to HTML', async () => {
    const { renderMarkdown } = await import('./markdown');
    const html = await renderMarkdown('# Hello\n\nparagraph');
    expect(html).toContain('<h1>Hello</h1>');
    expect(html).toContain('<p>paragraph</p>');
  });

  it('strips <script> tags (rehype-sanitize)', async () => {
    const { renderMarkdown } = await import('./markdown');
    const html = await renderMarkdown('ok\n\n<script>alert(1)</script>');
    expect(html).not.toContain('<script>');
    expect(html).not.toContain('alert(1)');
  });

  it('strips javascript: URLs from links', async () => {
    const { renderMarkdown } = await import('./markdown');
    const html = await renderMarkdown('[click](javascript:alert(1))');
    expect(html).not.toContain('javascript:');
  });

  it('supports GitHub-flavored markdown (tables)', async () => {
    const { renderMarkdown } = await import('./markdown');
    const html = await renderMarkdown('| a | b |\n|---|---|\n| 1 | 2 |');
    expect(html).toContain('<table>');
    expect(html).toContain('<td>1</td>');
  });

  it('highlights fenced code blocks via shiki', async () => {
    const { renderMarkdown } = await import('./markdown');
    const html = await renderMarkdown('```js\nconst x = 1;\n```');
    expect(html).toMatch(/<pre[^>]*class="[^"]*shiki/);
  });
});
```

- [ ] **Step 4.2: Run tests, confirm failure**

```bash
cd web/sites/packages
pnpm test
```

Expected: 5 tests FAIL with `Cannot find module './markdown'`.

- [ ] **Step 4.3: Implement `renderMarkdown`**

Create `web/sites/packages/src/lib/markdown.ts`:

```ts
import { unified } from 'unified';
import remarkParse from 'remark-parse';
import remarkGfm from 'remark-gfm';
import remarkRehype from 'remark-rehype';
import rehypeSanitize, { defaultSchema } from 'rehype-sanitize';
import rehypeShiki from '@shikijs/rehype';
import rehypeStringify from 'rehype-stringify';

// Relax the sanitizer schema to keep shiki's syntax-highlighting
// class names (which default-schema strips from <pre>/<code>).
const schema = {
  ...defaultSchema,
  attributes: {
    ...defaultSchema.attributes,
    pre: [...(defaultSchema.attributes?.pre ?? []), 'className', 'style'],
    code: [...(defaultSchema.attributes?.code ?? []), 'className', 'style'],
    span: [...(defaultSchema.attributes?.span ?? []), 'className', 'style'],
  },
};

export async function renderMarkdown(src: string): Promise<string> {
  const file = await unified()
    .use(remarkParse)
    .use(remarkGfm)
    .use(remarkRehype, { allowDangerousHtml: false })
    .use(rehypeSanitize, schema)
    .use(rehypeShiki, { themes: { light: 'github-light', dark: 'github-dark' } })
    .use(rehypeStringify)
    .process(src);
  return String(file);
}
```

**Pipeline order rationale:** `rehype-sanitize` runs BEFORE `rehype-shiki` so the sanitizer has already stripped anything dangerous from the raw markdown's HTML before shiki adds its highlighting classes. The relaxed schema allows shiki's own class/style attributes to survive sanitization.

- [ ] **Step 4.4: Run tests**

```bash
pnpm test
```

Expected: all 5 markdown tests pass.

**If the shiki highlighting test fails:** the shiki plugin name / API may have drifted from what's shown. Check `node_modules/@shikijs/rehype/package.json` for the actual default export. If it's different, adjust the import line. The other 4 tests should still pass.

- [ ] **Step 4.5: Commit**

```bash
git add web/sites/packages/src/lib/markdown.ts web/sites/packages/src/lib/markdown.test.ts
git commit -m "feat(docs): add markdown.ts for sanitized README rendering"
```

## Task 5: Build listing page + PackageCard component

**Files:**
- Create: `web/sites/packages/src/components/PackageCard.astro`
- Modify: `web/sites/packages/src/pages/index.astro` (replacing the placeholder from Task 1)

### Steps

- [ ] **Step 5.1: Create PackageCard.astro**

`web/sites/packages/src/components/PackageCard.astro`:

```astro
---
import type { PackageSummary } from '../lib/registry';

interface Props {
  pkg: PackageSummary;
}

const { pkg } = Astro.props;
const homepage = /^https?:\/\//.test(pkg.homepage) ? pkg.homepage : '';
---

<article class="package-card" data-match="true" data-search={`${pkg.name} ${pkg.description} ${pkg.tags.join(' ')}`.toLowerCase()}>
  <header>
    <h2>
      <a href={`/${pkg.name}`}>{pkg.name}</a>
    </h2>
    <span class="version-pill">v{pkg.latestVersion}</span>
  </header>
  <p class="description">{pkg.description}</p>
  {pkg.tags.length > 0 && (
    <ul class="tags">
      {pkg.tags.map((tag) => <li><span class="tag-chip">{tag}</span></li>)}
    </ul>
  )}
  <div class="install">
    <code id={`install-${pkg.name}`}>wheels packages install {pkg.name}</code>
    <button
      type="button"
      class="copy-btn"
      data-target={`install-${pkg.name}`}
      aria-label={`Copy install command for ${pkg.name}`}
    >Copy</button>
  </div>
  {homepage && (
    <footer>
      <a href={homepage} target="_blank" rel="noopener">Homepage ↗</a>
    </footer>
  )}
</article>

<style>
  .package-card {
    border: 1px solid var(--color-border, #e2e2e2);
    border-radius: 8px;
    padding: 1.25rem;
    background: var(--color-surface, #fff);
    display: flex;
    flex-direction: column;
    gap: 0.75rem;
  }
  .package-card[data-match='false'] { display: none; }
  .package-card header {
    display: flex;
    justify-content: space-between;
    align-items: baseline;
    gap: 0.5rem;
  }
  .package-card h2 { margin: 0; font-size: 1.125rem; }
  .package-card h2 a { text-decoration: none; color: inherit; }
  .package-card h2 a:hover { text-decoration: underline; }
  .version-pill {
    font-size: 0.75rem;
    padding: 0.125rem 0.5rem;
    border-radius: 999px;
    background: var(--color-surface-alt, #f3f3f3);
    color: var(--color-text-muted, #555);
  }
  .description { margin: 0; color: var(--color-text-muted, #555); font-size: 0.95rem; }
  .tags { list-style: none; padding: 0; margin: 0; display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 0.375rem; }
  .tag-chip {
    font-size: 0.75rem;
    padding: 0.125rem 0.5rem;
    border-radius: 4px;
    background: var(--color-accent-soft, #eef);
    color: var(--color-accent, #335);
  }
  .install { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; }
  .install code {
    flex: 1;
    font-size: 0.8125rem;
    padding: 0.25rem 0.5rem;
    background: var(--color-surface-alt, #f3f3f3);
    border-radius: 4px;
    overflow-x: auto;
    white-space: nowrap;
  }
  .copy-btn {
    font-size: 0.8125rem;
    padding: 0.25rem 0.625rem;
    border: 1px solid var(--color-border, #e2e2e2);
    background: var(--color-surface, #fff);
    border-radius: 4px;
    cursor: pointer;
  }
  .copy-btn:hover { background: var(--color-surface-alt, #f3f3f3); }
  footer { margin-top: auto; }
  footer a { font-size: 0.8125rem; color: var(--color-text-muted, #555); }
</style>
```

**Tokens:** the `var(--color-*)` references use what's likely defined in `@wheels-dev/ui/styles/tokens.css`. If those custom properties don't exist, the fallback values apply (grey/neutral). Verify token names during impl by reading `web/packages/ui/src/styles/tokens.css` and matching — rename if the actual token names differ (they might be `--accent`, `--fg-muted`, etc.).

- [ ] **Step 5.2: Replace placeholder index.astro**

`web/sites/packages/src/pages/index.astro`:

```astro
---
import BaseLayout from '../layouts/BaseLayout.astro';
import PackageCard from '../components/PackageCard.astro';
import { listAll } from '../lib/registry';

const packages = await listAll();
---

<BaseLayout
  title="Wheels packages"
  description="Browse the Wheels packages registry — first-party and community packages for the Wheels framework."
>
  <section class="packages-page">
    <header class="page-header">
      <h1>Wheels packages</h1>
      <p class="subhead">
        First-party and community packages for the Wheels framework. Install with
        <code>wheels packages install &lt;name&gt;</code>.
      </p>
      <label class="filter">
        <span class="visually-hidden">Filter packages</span>
        <input
          id="package-filter"
          type="search"
          placeholder="Filter packages by name, description, or tag…"
          aria-label="Filter packages"
        />
      </label>
    </header>

    <div class="package-grid">
      {packages.map((pkg) => <PackageCard pkg={pkg} />)}
    </div>

    <p class="count-line">
      <span id="visible-count">{packages.length}</span> of {packages.length} packages
    </p>
  </section>
</BaseLayout>

<script>
  // Client-side filter + copy-button behavior.
  // Runs on every page load; Astro ships this to the browser as a plain script.

  const filterInput = document.getElementById('package-filter') as HTMLInputElement | null;
  const cards = document.querySelectorAll<HTMLElement>('.package-card');
  const countEl = document.getElementById('visible-count');

  function applyFilter(query: string) {
    const q = query.trim().toLowerCase();
    let visible = 0;
    cards.forEach((card) => {
      const haystack = card.dataset.search ?? '';
      const match = q === '' || haystack.includes(q);
      card.dataset.match = match ? 'true' : 'false';
      if (match) visible += 1;
    });
    if (countEl) countEl.textContent = String(visible);
  }

  filterInput?.addEventListener('input', (e) => {
    applyFilter((e.target as HTMLInputElement).value);
  });

  // Copy buttons — 2s "Copied!" feedback.
  document.querySelectorAll<HTMLButtonElement>('.copy-btn').forEach((btn) => {
    btn.addEventListener('click', async () => {
      const targetId = btn.dataset.target;
      if (!targetId) return;
      const target = document.getElementById(targetId);
      if (!target) return;
      const text = target.textContent ?? '';
      try {
        await navigator.clipboard.writeText(text);
        const original = btn.textContent;
        btn.textContent = 'Copied!';
        setTimeout(() => { btn.textContent = original; }, 2000);
      } catch (_err) {
        // Older browsers without the clipboard API silently skip.
      }
    });
  });
</script>

<style>
  .packages-page { max-width: 72rem; margin: 0 auto; padding: 2rem 1rem 4rem; }
  .page-header { margin-bottom: 2rem; }
  .page-header h1 { margin: 0 0 0.5rem; font-size: 2rem; }
  .subhead { margin: 0 0 1.5rem; color: var(--color-text-muted, #555); max-width: 48rem; }
  .subhead code {
    font-size: 0.875em;
    background: var(--color-surface-alt, #f3f3f3);
    padding: 0.125rem 0.375rem;
    border-radius: 4px;
  }
  .filter input {
    width: 100%;
    max-width: 32rem;
    padding: 0.5rem 0.75rem;
    font-size: 1rem;
    border: 1px solid var(--color-border, #e2e2e2);
    border-radius: 6px;
  }
  .package-grid {
    display: grid;
    grid-template-columns: 1fr;
    gap: 1rem;
  }
  @media (min-width: 640px) {
    .package-grid { grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr); }
  }
  @media (min-width: 960px) {
    .package-grid { grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr); }
  }
  .count-line { margin-top: 1.5rem; color: var(--color-text-muted, #555); font-size: 0.875rem; }
  .visually-hidden {
    position: absolute;
    width: 1px;
    height: 1px;
    padding: 0;
    margin: -1px;
    overflow: hidden;
    clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0);
    white-space: nowrap;
    border: 0;
  }
</style>
```

```bash
cd web
pnpm --filter @wheels-dev/site-packages build
```

Expected: build succeeds. The frontmatter `await listAll()` fetches the live registry; GitHub should return 200 for all requests. Four packages (wheels-sentry, wheels-hotwire, wheels-basecoat, wheels-legacy-adapter) should be present in `dist/index.html`.

**If the build fails with a GitHub rate-limit error:** either set `WHEELS_PACKAGES_REGISTRY` to a local mock, or retry after the rate-limit window. The CI build uses the default github-actions IP pool which has higher limits.

```bash
grep -c "wheels-" dist/index.html
grep "wheels packages install" dist/index.html | head -4
```

Expected: at least 4 mentions of "wheels-" (one per package), 4 install snippets.

```bash
git commit -m "feat(docs): add listing page + PackageCard for packages site"
```

## Task 6: Build detail page

**Files:**
- Create: `web/sites/packages/src/pages/[name].astro`

### Steps

- [ ] **Step 6.1: Create `[name].astro`**

`web/sites/packages/src/pages/[name].astro`:

```astro
---
import BaseLayout from '../layouts/BaseLayout.astro';
import { listPackageNames, fetchManifest, fetchReadme, type Manifest } from '../lib/registry';
import { renderMarkdown } from '../lib/markdown';

export async function getStaticPaths() {
  const names = await listPackageNames();
  return Promise.all(
    names.map(async (name) => {
      const manifest = await fetchManifest(name);
      const readmeSource = await fetchReadme(name).catch(() => '');
      const readmeHtml = readmeSource ? await renderMarkdown(readmeSource) : '';
      return {
        params: { name },
        props: { manifest, readmeHtml },
      };
    }),
  );
}

interface Props {
  manifest: Manifest;
  readmeHtml: string;
}

const { manifest, readmeHtml } = Astro.props;
const latest = manifest.versions[manifest.versions.length - 1];
const homepage = manifest.homepage && /^https?:\/\//.test(manifest.homepage)
  ? manifest.homepage
  : '';
// Reverse for display — newest first.
const versionsDesc = [...manifest.versions].reverse();
---

<BaseLayout
  title={`${manifest.name} — Wheels packages`}
  description={manifest.description}
>
  <article class="package-detail">
    <nav class="breadcrumb" aria-label="Breadcrumb">
      <a href="/">Packages</a>
      <span aria-hidden="true"> / </span>
      <span>{manifest.name}</span>
    </nav>

    <header>
      <h1>{manifest.name}</h1>
      <span class="version-pill">v{latest.version}</span>
    </header>

    {manifest.description && <p class="description">{manifest.description}</p>}

    {manifest.tags && manifest.tags.length > 0 && (
      <ul class="tags" aria-label="Tags">
        {manifest.tags.map((tag) => <li><span class="tag-chip">{tag}</span></li>)}
      </ul>
    )}

    {homepage && (
      <p class="homepage">
        <a href={homepage} target="_blank" rel="noopener">Homepage ↗</a>
      </p>
    )}

    <section class="install-section">
      <h2>Install</h2>
      <div class="install">
        <code id="install-latest">wheels packages install {manifest.name}</code>
        <button type="button" class="copy-btn" data-target="install-latest" aria-label={`Copy latest install command for ${manifest.name}`}>Copy</button>
      </div>
      <div class="install">
        <code id="install-pinned">wheels packages install {manifest.name}@{latest.version}</code>
        <button type="button" class="copy-btn" data-target="install-pinned" aria-label={`Copy pinned install command for ${manifest.name}`}>Copy</button>
      </div>
    </section>

    <section class="versions-section">
      <h2>Version history</h2>
      <table class="versions-table">
        <thead>
          <tr>
            <th>Version</th>
            <th>Published</th>
            <th>Requires Wheels</th>
            <th>sha256</th>
          </tr>
        </thead>
        <tbody>
          {versionsDesc.map((v) => (
            <tr>
              <td><code>{v.version}</code></td>
              <td><time datetime={v.publishedAt}>{v.publishedAt.slice(0, 10)}</time></td>
              <td><code>{v.wheelsVersion}</code></td>
              <td>
                <code title={v.sha256} class="sha">{v.sha256.slice(0, 12)}…</code>
              </td>
            </tr>
          ))}
        </tbody>
      </table>
    </section>

    {readmeHtml && (
      <section class="readme-section">
        <h2>README</h2>
        <article class="prose" set:html={readmeHtml} />
      </section>
    )}
  </article>
</BaseLayout>

<script>
  document.querySelectorAll<HTMLButtonElement>('.copy-btn').forEach((btn) => {
    btn.addEventListener('click', async () => {
      const targetId = btn.dataset.target;
      if (!targetId) return;
      const target = document.getElementById(targetId);
      if (!target) return;
      const text = target.textContent ?? '';
      try {
        await navigator.clipboard.writeText(text);
        const original = btn.textContent;
        btn.textContent = 'Copied!';
        setTimeout(() => { btn.textContent = original; }, 2000);
      } catch (_err) {
        // No-op for older browsers.
      }
    });
  });
</script>

<style>
  .package-detail { max-width: 64rem; margin: 0 auto; padding: 2rem 1rem 4rem; }
  .breadcrumb { font-size: 0.875rem; color: var(--color-text-muted, #555); margin-bottom: 1rem; }
  .breadcrumb a { color: inherit; }
  .package-detail header { display: flex; align-items: baseline; gap: 0.75rem; margin-bottom: 0.5rem; }
  .package-detail h1 { margin: 0; font-size: 2rem; }
  .version-pill {
    font-size: 0.875rem;
    padding: 0.125rem 0.625rem;
    border-radius: 999px;
    background: var(--color-surface-alt, #f3f3f3);
    color: var(--color-text-muted, #555);
  }
  .description { font-size: 1.125rem; color: var(--color-text-muted, #555); margin: 0 0 1rem; }
  .tags { list-style: none; padding: 0; margin: 0 0 1rem; display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 0.375rem; }
  .tag-chip {
    font-size: 0.75rem;
    padding: 0.125rem 0.5rem;
    border-radius: 4px;
    background: var(--color-accent-soft, #eef);
    color: var(--color-accent, #335);
  }
  .homepage { margin: 0 0 2rem; font-size: 0.9375rem; }
  .install-section, .versions-section, .readme-section { margin-top: 2.5rem; }
  .install-section h2, .versions-section h2, .readme-section h2 {
    font-size: 1.25rem; margin-bottom: 0.75rem;
  }
  .install { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; margin-bottom: 0.5rem; }
  .install code {
    flex: 1;
    font-size: 0.875rem;
    padding: 0.375rem 0.625rem;
    background: var(--color-surface-alt, #f3f3f3);
    border-radius: 4px;
    overflow-x: auto;
    white-space: nowrap;
  }
  .copy-btn {
    font-size: 0.875rem;
    padding: 0.375rem 0.75rem;
    border: 1px solid var(--color-border, #e2e2e2);
    background: var(--color-surface, #fff);
    border-radius: 4px;
    cursor: pointer;
  }
  .copy-btn:hover { background: var(--color-surface-alt, #f3f3f3); }
  .versions-table {
    width: 100%;
    border-collapse: collapse;
    font-size: 0.875rem;
  }
  .versions-table th, .versions-table td {
    text-align: left;
    padding: 0.5rem 0.75rem;
    border-bottom: 1px solid var(--color-border, #e2e2e2);
  }
  .versions-table th { font-weight: 600; color: var(--color-text-muted, #555); }
  .sha { cursor: help; }
  .prose { max-width: 48rem; }
</style>
```

```bash
pnpm --filter @wheels-dev/site-packages build
```

```bash
ls dist/ | grep -v "^index\|^favicon\|^robots\|^sitemap\|^_astro"
```

## Task 7: Wire the deploy workflow

**Files:**
- Modify: `.github/workflows/web-deploy.yml`

### Steps

- [ ] **Step 7.1: Add `repository_dispatch` trigger and extend matrix**

Open `.github/workflows/web-deploy.yml`. Find the `on:` block at the top:

```yaml
on:
  push:
    branches: [develop]
    paths:
      - 'web/**'
      - '.github/workflows/web-deploy.yml'
  pull_request:
    branches: [develop]
    paths:
      - 'web/**'
      - '.github/workflows/web-deploy.yml'
```

Add `repository_dispatch`:

```yaml
on:
  push:
    branches: [develop]
    paths:
      - 'web/**'
      - '.github/workflows/web-deploy.yml'
  pull_request:
    branches: [develop]
    paths:
      - 'web/**'
      - '.github/workflows/web-deploy.yml'
  repository_dispatch:
    types: [registry-updated]
```

Find the matrix block:

```yaml
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        site: [landing, blog, guides, api]
```

Change to:

```yaml
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        site: ${{ github.event_name == 'repository_dispatch' && fromJSON('["packages"]') || fromJSON('["landing", "blog", "guides", "api", "packages"]') }}
```

- [ ] **Step 7.2: Validate YAML syntax**

```bash
python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/web-deploy.yml'))"
```

Expected: no output (success). If YAML parsing fails, the ternary expression probably needs to be on its own line or the quotes escaped differently.

- [ ] **Step 7.3: Validate with actionlint (if available)**

```bash
which actionlint && actionlint .github/workflows/web-deploy.yml
```

If `actionlint` isn't installed, skip this step. GitHub's own parser will validate on push.

- [ ] **Step 7.4: Commit**

```bash
git add .github/workflows/web-deploy.yml
git commit -m "feat(docs): wire packages site into web-deploy matrix with registry dispatch"
```

## Task 8: Capture visual regression baselines

**Files:**
- Create: `web/tests/visual-baselines/packages-index.png` (generated)
- Create: `web/tests/visual-baselines/packages-detail.png` (generated)

The `pnpm visual:test` harness compares rendered screenshots against committed baselines. First run for a new site has no baseline → test fails → download diffs → commit the `.actual.png` files as baselines.

### Steps

- [ ] **Step 8.1: Inspect `web/scripts/visual-regression.mjs` to understand site enumeration**

```bash
cat web/scripts/visual-regression.mjs | head -80
```

Look for how the script enumerates which sites to screenshot. If it's a hardcoded array (e.g., `const SITES = ['landing', 'blog', ...]`), add `'packages'` to it. If it auto-discovers from `web/sites/*`, no edit needed.

- [ ] **Step 8.2: If the script needs an edit, add `packages` to the site list**

(Exact edit depends on the script's structure — inspect and mimic the existing pattern. Commit this edit with the baseline capture in step 8.5.)

- [ ] **Step 8.3: Run `pnpm visual:baseline` to capture new baselines**

```bash
cd web
pnpm visual:baseline
```

Expected: the `--update` mode writes new baseline PNGs (including for `packages/index` and `packages/wheels-sentry` — or whichever pages the harness decides to capture). All existing baselines re-capture as well, which is fine if they're unchanged.

- [ ] **Step 8.4: Inspect what got written**

```bash
git status web/tests/visual-baselines/
```

Expected: new `packages-*.png` files. Existing site baselines may show as modified — revert those if the diff is unrelated to this change (unlikely but check with `git diff`; image diffs show in git only as "binary files differ", use `git diff --stat`).

```bash
# If existing baselines changed unexpectedly, revert them.
git checkout -- web/tests/visual-baselines/landing-*.png \
                 web/tests/visual-baselines/blog-*.png \
                 web/tests/visual-baselines/guides-*.png \
                 web/tests/visual-baselines/api-*.png 2>/dev/null || true
```

- [ ] **Step 8.5: Verify `pnpm visual:test` passes with the new baselines**

```bash
pnpm visual:test
```

Expected: all sites pass, no diffs.

- [ ] **Step 8.6: Commit**

```bash
git add web/tests/visual-baselines/packages-*.png
# If the regression script needed edits:
git add web/scripts/visual-regression.mjs
git commit -m "test(docs): add visual-regression baselines for packages site"
```

---

## Task 9: Cross-repo — notify workflow in `wheels-dev/wheels-packages`

**This task runs in a different repo** — `wheels-dev/wheels-packages`, not `wheels-dev/wheels`. Clone or `cd` into that repo first. This task becomes its own PR against that repo's `main` branch.

**Files:**
- Create: `.github/workflows/notify-site.yml`
- Modify: `CONTRIBUTING.md` (append a "Site rebuild trigger" section)

### Prerequisites (one-time manual, done outside this plan)

1. Create a fine-grained personal access token:
   - Resource owner: `wheels-dev`
   - Repository access: **Only select repositories** → `wheels-dev/wheels`
   - Permissions → Repository → **Contents: Read and write** (required for `repository_dispatch`)
   - Expiration: long-lived (1 year) — recurring rotation should be on the team's calendar
2. In the `wheels-dev/wheels-packages` repo settings → Secrets and variables → Actions → New repository secret:
   - Name: `NOTIFY_WHEELS_TOKEN`
   - Value: the token from step 1
3. Verify the secret exists (no value visible, but the name should appear in the list).

### Steps

- [ ] **Step 9.1: Clone or navigate to `wheels-dev/wheels-packages`**

```bash
cd ~/GitHub/wheels-dev
# Adjust path if your local clone is elsewhere.
if [ ! -d wheels-packages ]; then
  gh repo clone wheels-dev/wheels-packages
fi
cd wheels-packages
git checkout main
git pull
git checkout -b peter/notify-site-on-merge
```

- [ ] **Step 9.2: Create `.github/workflows/notify-site.yml`**

```yaml
name: Notify wheels.dev site

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
    paths:
      - 'packages/**'
      - 'schema/**'

jobs:
  notify:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Fire repository_dispatch at wheels-dev/wheels
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NOTIFY_WHEELS_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          gh api repos/wheels-dev/wheels/dispatches \
            --method POST \
            --field event_type=registry-updated \
            --field "client_payload[source_commit]=${GITHUB_SHA}"
```

- [ ] **Step 9.3: Validate YAML**

```bash
python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/notify-site.yml'))"
```

Expected: no output (success).

- [ ] **Step 9.4: Append to CONTRIBUTING.md**

If `CONTRIBUTING.md` doesn't yet have a "Site rebuild" section, append at the end:

```markdown

## Site rebuild trigger

Merging a PR that changes `packages/**` or `schema/**` on `main` automatically
rebuilds [packages.wheels.dev](https://packages.wheels.dev) via a
`repository_dispatch` event fired at `wheels-dev/wheels`. The trigger is
implemented in [`.github/workflows/notify-site.yml`](.github/workflows/notify-site.yml)
and requires a repository secret named `NOTIFY_WHEELS_TOKEN` — a fine-grained
PAT scoped to `wheels-dev/wheels` with `contents: write`.

If a registry merge does not rebuild the site within a few minutes:
1. Check the `Notify wheels.dev site` workflow run in this repo's Actions tab.
2. If it succeeded, check the `Deploy static sites` workflow in `wheels-dev/wheels`.
3. If the notify workflow failed with a 401/403, the PAT has likely expired — rotate and update the secret.
```

- [ ] **Step 9.5: Commit + push + open PR**

```bash
git add .github/workflows/notify-site.yml CONTRIBUTING.md
git commit -m "feat: rebuild packages.wheels.dev on manifest merges"
git push -u origin peter/notify-site-on-merge
gh pr create --base main --title "feat: rebuild packages.wheels.dev on manifest merges" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Fires a `repository_dispatch` event at `wheels-dev/wheels` on every push to `main` that touches `packages/**` or `schema/**`
- Pairs with the matching trigger in `wheels-dev/wheels/.github/workflows/web-deploy.yml` (see wheels-dev/wheels PR for the packages site)
- Requires repo secret `NOTIFY_WHEELS_TOKEN` — fine-grained PAT, scoped to `wheels-dev/wheels`, `contents: write`

## Test plan
- [ ] Merge this PR
- [ ] Open and merge a trivial manifest touch (e.g., whitespace) to confirm the notify workflow runs and the `wheels-dev/wheels` deploy workflow is triggered
- [ ] Check `packages.wheels.dev` reflects the change within ~3 minutes
EOF
)"
```

- [ ] **Step 9.6: Return to the wheels repo worktree**

```bash
cd -   # or `cd /Users/peter/GitHub/wheels-dev/wheels/.claude/worktrees/<worktree>`
```

---

## Task 10: Open the wheels repo PR

**Files:** none modified in this task — this is the PR for all prior commits.

### Steps

- [ ] **Step 10.1: Push the branch**

```bash
git push -u origin peter/packages-site-design
```

(Or whatever branch name the worktree is on; this plan was drafted on `peter/packages-site-design`.)

- [ ] **Step 10.2: Open the PR against `develop`**

```bash
gh pr create --base develop --title "feat(docs): packages.wheels.dev static site (#2271)" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
Adds `packages.wheels.dev` as a fifth Astro static site alongside `wheels.dev` / `blog.wheels.dev` / `guides.wheels.dev` / `api.wheels.dev`. Fed by the `wheels-dev/wheels-packages` registry at build time; rebuilt on registry merges via `repository_dispatch` (see companion PR in the registry repo).

Closes the wheels.dev half of #2271. (The in-app `/wheels/packages` half shipped in #2277.)

## Changes
- `web/sites/packages/` — new Astro site (listing page with card grid + client-side filter, detail pages with version history and rendered README)
- `web/vitest.config.ts` + root `test` script — first unit-test runner in `web/` (`vitest`)
- `web/sites/packages/src/lib/registry.ts` + tests — build-time fetcher
- `web/sites/packages/src/lib/markdown.ts` + tests — `unified`/`remark`/`rehype-sanitize`/`rehype-shiki` pipeline
- `.github/workflows/web-deploy.yml` — add `packages` to matrix, add `repository_dispatch` trigger, gate registry-triggered runs to `packages` only
- `web/tests/visual-baselines/packages-*.png` — new baselines

## One-time prep required before this PR merges
- [ ] **Cloudflare Pages project `wheels-packages` created, `packages.wheels.dev` custom domain attached.** Without this, the `wrangler pages deploy` step errors on first run. This is a manual CF dashboard step; no way to automate in-repo.

## Prep that can happen after this PR merges
- Companion PR in `wheels-dev/wheels-packages` adds `notify-site.yml` + `NOTIFY_WHEELS_TOKEN` secret. Until that lands, the packages site rebuilds only on `web/**` changes in this repo — registry merges won't auto-refresh it. Harmless intermediate state.

## Test plan
- [x] Unit tests pass: `cd web/sites/packages && pnpm test`
- [x] Build succeeds against live registry: `pnpm --filter @wheels-dev/site-packages build`
- [x] Visual regression baselines captured and `pnpm visual:test` green
- [ ] CI full matrix green (waiting on PR CI)
- [ ] After CF project setup + merge: `packages.wheels.dev` loads, all 4 current packages appear
- [ ] After companion wheels-packages PR merges: trivial manifest touch triggers a rebuild within ~3 min

## Spec & plan
- Spec: [`docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-23-wheels-packages-phase4-site-design.md`](docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-23-wheels-packages-phase4-site-design.md)
- Plan: [`docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-23-wheels-packages-phase4-site.md`](docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-23-wheels-packages-phase4-site.md)

Refs #2271, #2243
EOF
)"
```

- [ ] **Step 10.3: Wait for CI green (code tier per `CLAUDE.local.md`)**

This is a code-tier PR (touches `web/`, `.github/workflows/`). Wait for the **full** non-skipped check suite to be `pass`. Treat `pending`/`queued`/`in_progress` as not done. Treat `fail`/`cancelled` as a stop.

Poll with:
```bash
gh pr checks
```

- [ ] **Step 10.4: Merge once green**

```bash
gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch
```

Report the merge commit SHA and confirm branch deletion.

---

## Self-review notes (executed during plan writing)

- **Spec coverage:** Every spec component (1–7) maps to a plan task. Site skeleton (spec §1) → Task 1. Registry data layer (spec §2) → Task 3. Markdown rendering (spec §3) → Task 4. Listing page (spec §4) → Task 5. Detail page (spec §5) → Task 6. Deploy workflow edits (spec §6) → Task 7. Notify workflow (spec §7) → Task 9. Testing (spec "Testing") → vitest setup in Task 2 + TDD in Tasks 3/4 + visual baselines in Task 8. Security (spec "Security") → homepage scheme whitelist in PackageCard/detail, `rehype-sanitize` in markdown.ts, env-var-only registry override in registry.ts.
- **Placeholder scan:** No TBDs. Every code block is complete. One intentional "verify during impl" note (token names in `@wheels-dev/ui/styles/tokens.css`) because that file's exact contents aren't visible to the plan writer; the fallback values in the CSS rules cover the uncertainty.
- **Type consistency:** `Manifest`, `PackageSummary`, and `ManifestVersion` are declared once in `registry.ts` and imported wherever needed. Property names (`latestVersion`, `publishedAt`, `homepage`, `tags`) are consistent between `registry.ts` (output), `PackageCard.astro` (consumer), and `[name].astro` (consumer of full `Manifest`). No drift.
- **Deviation from spec:** Spec proposed `4325` dev port assuming landing/blog/guides/api were at 4321/4322/4323/4324. Verified during writing — matches. No change.
- **Deviation from spec:** Spec mentioned `@astrojs/sitemap` integration. Kept in Task 1. No separate sitemap task needed — integration auto-generates on build.
